feed.title: SAP Community - SAP AI Services
feed.subtitle: SAP AI Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Issue in SAP generative AI Hub python SDK.
entry.content:
While using the sap generative ai hub python sdk to utilize the AI models like GPT 4o, there seems to be an issue regarding the calls. I have utilized the API calls in a loop such that mutiple calls are made one after the other, but the issue I face is that suddenly after some calls, the application just get stuck at fetching the output results. And the reason is unclear as its just random when this happens. Seems no error in the code as there are no exceptions raised as well. It just stops fetching at any random point in the loop. Its true when using embedding models as well. I did go through the documentation for the SDK but found nothing relevant. A help would be appreciated. The application is hosted in SAP BTP cloud foundry environment

 Python   #SAPGenerativeAIHubSDk #SAPAICORE

entry.title: SAP A.I. Privacy and Security
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

An important factor with generative A.I. is security - Does our data we submit to an A.I. train a model and potentially thereby expose confidential information?

AWS Bedrock is an A.I. service offered by Amazon and they clearly state that it is secure and private. It is safe to submit confidential information.

SAP A.I. Launchpad / Generative A.I. Hub offers access to many models - are these models hosted on SAP servers or does it just connect to the A.I. providers servers?

We're looking for a definitive answer that submitting confidential/sensitive data to a model on SAP A.I. Launchpad is safe, and that our data is not exposed to the public internet or used to train models?

Kind Regards,

Reinhardt

entry.title: SAC Compass - any instructions available?
entry.content:
Hi community,

its great that compass is now available in our SAC Test Tenant, I am looking forward to using it!

Is there any instructional material available at this point? I havent found any on SAC Help Portal, SAC Community, SAP Youtube or directly in SAC.

Please let me know if there is any!

Kind regards,

Eric

entry.title: SAP HANA Rev 59.16: Driving Innovation with Enhanced Performance, Security, and Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi SAP HANA Experts, 

I want to create a blog in the portala for powerful in-memory data platform, continues to evolve and play a crucial role in the digital transformation journey for businesses worldwide. The latest release, SPS 05, which reached Revision 59.16 on January 7, 2025, brings a wealth of new features and enhancements aimed at improving performance, security, cloud integration, and user experience. Let’s take a closer look at what’s new in this release and how businesses can benefit from it.

  1. Enhanced Performance and Speed

SAP HANA consistently focuses on improving its core performance, and the Rev 59.16 release continues this trend with significant advancements:

  • Optimized Query Processing: The speed of query execution has been significantly boosted, thanks to improvements in internal memory management. This ensures faster response times, even with complex workloads.
  • Improved Data Tiering: Better data tiering now allows SAP HANA to efficiently manage hot, warm, and cold data. This results in improved system performance, particularly for organizations dealing with massive volumes of data.

These enhancements ensure SAP HANA remains a high-performance solution for real-time analytics, no matter the scale or complexity of the data involved.

  1. Cloud Integration and Hybrid Enhancements

As businesses increasingly embrace hybrid and multi-cloud environments, Rev 59.16 makes it easier for SAP HANA to integrate with various cloud platforms:

  • Cloud-Native Capabilities: The release introduces enhanced cloud-native features, allowing businesses to seamlessly run SAP HANA applications both on-premise and in the cloud, offering greater flexibility.
  • SAP HANA Cloud Integration: Stronger integration tools simplify bridging on-premise SAP HANA with SAP HANA Cloud, creating a smoother hybrid cloud experience and enabling efficient management of data across multiple environments.

These features support a more unified, efficient, and scalable cloud experience for businesses managing their data in a hybrid or multi-cloud infrastructure.

  1. Security Improvements

SAP HANA Rev 59.16 prioritizes enhanced security, addressing the growing concerns around data protection and compliance:

  • Enhanced Data Encryption: Stronger encryption features have been introduced to safeguard sensitive data both in transit and at rest. This ensures that businesses meet stringent security and compliance standards, particularly in industries with high data sensitivity.
  • Stronger Authentication: The system now supports a wider array of authentication protocols, improving access control and security for both users and services interacting with SAP HANA.

These security upgrades ensure that SAP HANA remains a trusted platform for businesses dealing with highly sensitive data and regulatory requirements.

  1. AI and Machine Learning Integration

SAP HANA has also made significant strides in integrating AI and machine learning capabilities, making it easier for businesses to harness the power of advanced analytics:

  • Streamlined AI Integration: The new release enhances integration with AI and machine learning frameworks, enabling businesses to run predictive models directly on their SAP HANA data. This reduces the time between data collection and actionable insights, allowing organizations to make smarter decisions faster.
  • Graph Data Processing: The expansion of graph data processing allows businesses to analyze complex relationships within their data more effectively. This feature is especially valuable in sectors like finance and telecommunications, where understanding intricate data relationships is critical.

These capabilities enable organizations to leverage the full potential of their data and unlock deeper insights using AI and machine learning.

  1. Improved Application Development

For developers working on SAP applications, Rev 59.16 introduces several new tools and features aimed at improving the development experience:

  • New Development Frameworks: The release includes additional development frameworks designed to speed up the application lifecycle, helping developers build and deploy applications more efficiently.

Thanks,

Divya Sahukari

entry.title: Does anyone know where I can download compass example model?
entry.content:
In the SAC documentation "Create a Simulation from Compass Start Page" in the Simulation Settings dialog, "select a model", the model selection says, "Compass Example Model".
Does anyone know where I can download this model?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: AI-Powered Predictive Maintenance with SAP: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced world, manufacturing industries are leveraging the power of AI to improve equipment reliability and optimize maintenance costs. SAP’s robust ecosystem is playing a vital role in transforming predictive maintenance through a blend of AI, IoT, and machine learning technologies. This post explores how to implement an AI-driven predictive maintenance system using SAP technologies like SAP Leonardo IoT, SAP HANA, SAP AI Core, and SAP Predictive Asset Insights (PAI).

1. Data Collection Layer – IoT Sensor Integration

The foundation of predictive maintenance lies in data collection. SAP Leonardo IoT plays a crucial role by collecting real-time sensor data from connected machines, including parameters such as temperature, vibration, and pressure. With protocols like MQTT, OPC UA, and REST API, IoT sensors are easily integrated into SAP's platform.

Key Components:

  • SAP Leonardo IoT: Collects and processes sensor data in real-time.
  • Protocols Supported: MQTT, OPC UA, and REST API for seamless connectivity.
  • Edge Processing: SAP Edge Services can preprocess data on-site before sending it to the cloud, ensuring faster insights.

    2. Data Processing & AI/ML Layer – Predictive Analysis

    Once the data is collected, the next step is to process it and apply AI to predict equipment failures. SAP HANA Machine Learning (ML) offers a native library for predictive analysis, including the Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) and Automated Predictive Library (APL). These tools allow you to build AI models that can predict future equipment failures based on historical data.

    Key Tools:

    • SAP HANA ML (PAL, APL): Enables the creation of machine learning models directly within SAP HANA.
    • SAP AI Core & AI Foundation: Leverages advanced deep learning models for precise predictions.
    • SAP Data Intelligence: Provides ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) functionality to clean and prepare IoT data for training AI models.

      3. Integration & Action Layer – SAP PM Automation

      The true value of predictive maintenance is realized when you integrate predictive analytics with maintenance workflows. SAP’s integration tools such as SAP Predictive Asset Insights (PAI) and SAP S/4HANA PM automate the process of creating work orders based on AI-driven predictions.

      Key Components:

      • SAP Predictive Asset Insights (PAI): Analyzes AI predictions and integrates them into SAP PM.
      • SAP S/4HANA PM: Triggers automated work orders based on failure predictions.
      • SAP Fiori Apps: User-friendly dashboards for managing maintenance orders, providing a real-time view of equipment performance and AI predictions.

        4. Machine Learning Model Implementation in SAP HANA

        SAP HANA’s native ML capabilities allow you to create predictive models for failure prediction. For instance, using the Random Forest algorithm, you can predict the likelihood of failure based on historical data.

        Step 1: Train the Predictive Model Using SAP HANA PAL

        Here’s an example SQL script to train a Random Forest model in SAP HANA:

         

        CALL "_SYS_AFL"."RANDOM_FOREST_TRAIN" ( 'TRAINING_TABLE', 'TARGET_COLUMN', 'PREDICTED_COLUMN', 'MODEL_STORAGE_TABLE' );

        This model uses historical failure data to predict future failures, storing the trained model in HANA’s high-speed columnar tables for quick predictions.

        Step 2: Predict Equipment Failures in Real-Time

        Once the model is trained, you can use it to predict failures in real-time based on incoming sensor data:

         

        CALL "_SYS_AFL"."RANDOM_FOREST_PREDICT" ( 'LIVE_SENSOR_DATA', 'MODEL_STORAGE_TABLE', 'PREDICTION_RESULTS' );

        The results are then stored in SAP Predictive Asset Insights (PAI) for further analysis.

        Step 3: Trigger Automated Maintenance Orders in SAP PM

        When a failure is predicted, SAP PM can automatically create a maintenance work order. The BAPI_ALM_ORDER_MAINTAIN function is used to integrate predictive analytics with SAP PM:

        CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_ALM_ORDER_MAINTAIN' EXPORTING ORDER_HEADER = order_header TABLES RETURN = return_messages.

         

        This process ensures that maintenance is triggered automatically based on predictive insights, reducing the need for manual intervention.

        5. SAP Leonardo IoT Configuration for Predictive Maintenance

        To connect IoT sensors with SAP HANA, you can configure IoT data ingestion via SAP Leonardo IoT. Data from connected sensors is transmitted using protocols such as MQTT, ensuring seamless integration with SAP's cloud platform.

        Here’s an example of a REST API request to send sensor data to SAP Leonardo IoT:

         

        This sends real-time data from equipment sensors to SAP Leonardo IoT, where it can be processed for predictive analysis.

        POST /sap/iot/thing HTTP/1.1 Host: iot-sap.com Content-Type: application/json { "deviceId": "MOTOR-123", "temperature": 85, "vibration": 0.12, "pressure": 5.6 }

      •  

        6. SAP PM & AI Integration – End-to-End Predictive Workflow

        With the integration of SAP’s tools, a typical predictive maintenance workflow looks like this:

        IoT Sensors Collect Data: Real-time data such as temperature, vibration, and pressure is collected by SAP Leonardo IoT.

      •  

        1. Machine Learning Model Predicts Failures: SAP HANA ML (PAL) uses historical data to predict equipment failures.
        2. Automated SAP PM Work Orders: Based on predictions, SAP PM automatically creates maintenance work orders.
        3. Technicians Perform Maintenance: Using SAP Fiori apps, technicians can view work orders and receive real-time alerts for proactive maintenance.

          7. SAP Predictive Asset Insights (PAI) – Configuration & T-Codes

          SAP PAI integrates predictive maintenance capabilities with SAP S/4HANA for asset management. The following T-codes can be used to manage and monitor predictive maintenance tasks:

          T-Code Function
          IL03Display Equipment Master DataIW31Create Maintenance Order (Auto-triggered by AI)IW32Change Maintenance OrderIW39List Maintenance OrdersIQ01Create Equipment Serial NumberIW67Display Notifications

          8. SAP Fiori Dashboard – AI-Powered Equipment Monitoring

          SAP Fiori offers intuitive, real-time dashboards to monitor equipment health. Features include:

          • Anomaly detection: Identifies irregularities in temperature, pressure, or vibration.
          • Failure probability analysis: Displays the likelihood of failure using AI predictions.
          • Automated maintenance scheduling: Automatically triggers and manages maintenance tasks based on AI insights.

            9. Real-World Case Study – AI-Powered SAP PM for Manufacturing

            A global automotive manufacturer successfully implemented an AI-based predictive maintenance solution. They used:

            • SAP Leonardo IoT for real-time sensor data.
            • SAP HANA ML (PAL) for failure prediction.
            • SAP PAI for real-time anomaly detection.
            • SAP PM (IW31, IW39) to automate maintenance work orders.

              Results:

              • 30% reduction in maintenance costs.
              • 40% decrease in unplanned downtime.
              • Automation of work order creation in SAP PM.

                10. Conclusion – Why AI-Driven SAP Predictive Maintenance?

                The future of SAP PM lies in AI, IoT, and predictive analytics. By implementing these technologies, organizations can:

                • Transition from reactive to proactive maintenance.
                • Predict failures before they happen.
                • Automate SAP PM processes (IW31, IW39, IL03) to improve operational efficiency.
                • Realize cost savings through optimized maintenance scheduling.

                  AI-driven predictive maintenance with SAP is a game-changer, offering businesses better control over their assets and enhancing operational reliability. Interested in building an AI-powered predictive maintenance solution in your SAP landscape? Let’s discuss how on this chat!!!

                  This comprehensive guide combines AI, ML, SAP HANA, IoT, and SAP PM into a seamless predictive maintenance solution. Whether you’re looking for implementation steps or a prototype architecture, the potential to transform your maintenance strategy is within reach!

entry.title: Create a sample page with Visual Search component from Hybris Administration Console for CX
entry.content: While integrating CX AI Toolkit for spartacus, we have an issue regarding the above line where we have to create a visual search component in the backend from HAC.

entry.title: Getting Failed to get org and user error while doing visual search storefront integration
entry.content:
While attempting to use the CX AI Toolkit API Integration for visual search with access to CX tenant details, authorization was granted with a token in the header and image multipart file body. I encountered the following issue.

Failed to get org and user

Try It Out | SAP Business Accelerator Hub | Visual Search

entry.title: Creating a Multi - input and Multi -output model
entry.content:
SAP provides a wonderful service over BTP DAR(Document Attribute Recommender ) which allows multi input and multi output model . However some Financial Companies handles extremely sensitive financial data, including personal information and transaction details.
They might be concerned about data privacy and security and are often subject to strict regulatory requirements regarding data handling and storage. 
In such a Scenario when they do not want to transfer any data over any other platform but make it in house using the following steps.

Let's understand each stage of the code to achieve this feature.

1. Data Preparation

First, you need to prepare your data. Let's assume you have a dataset with both numerical and categorical features.

2. Preprocessing

You need to preprocess your data to convert categorical variables into numerical ones. This is done using encoders like "OneHotEncoder" for categorical features and StandardScaler for numerical features.

Both OneHotEncoder and StandardScaler are available in Python through the scikit-learn library.

You can install scikit-learn using pip 

pip install scikit-learn

3. Model Training

Next, you create a pipeline that includes both the preprocessing steps and the model. This ensures that the same transformations are applied during both training and inference.

4. Model Inference

For inference, you need to preprocess the input data in the same way as during training, make predictions, and then convert the predictions back to their original categorical values.

So here  is the entire coding

import pandas as pd
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder, StandardScaler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.multioutput import MultiOutputClassifier
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier

# Example input data
X_train = pd.DataFrame({
    'Company_code': ['A', 'B', 'A'],
    'Vendor': ['X', 'Y', 'Z'],
    'Amount': [100, 200, 150],
    'InvoiceDocumenttype': ['Type1', 'Type2', 'Type1'],
    'Fiscalyear': [2021, 2022, 2021]
})

# Example output data
y_train = pd.DataFrame({
    'HKONT': ['Account1', 'Account2', 'Account1'],
    'KOSTL': ['Cost1', 'Cost2', 'Cost1'],
    'Profitcenters': ['Center1', 'Center2', 'Center1'],
    'Paymentterms': ['Term1', 'Term2', 'Term1'],
    'Partnerbanktype': ['Bank1', 'Bank2', 'Bank1'],
    'Taxcode': ['Tax1', 'Tax2', 'Tax1']
})

# Preprocessing for numerical and categorical data
preprocessor = ColumnTransformer(
    transformers=[
        ('num', StandardScaler(), ['Amount', 'Fiscalyear']),
        ('cat', OneHotEncoder(), ['Company_code', 'Vendor', 'InvoiceDocumenttype'])
    ])

# Multi-output model
model = Pipeline(steps=[
    ('preprocessor', preprocessor),
    ('classifier', MultiOutputClassifier(DecisionTreeClassifier()))
])

# Fit the model
model.fit(X_train, y_train)

# Example input for prediction
X_test = pd.DataFrame({
    'Company_code': ['A'],
    'Vendor': ['X'],
    'Amount': [120],
    'InvoiceDocumenttype': ['Type1'],
    'Fiscalyear': [2021]
})

# Predict
predictions = model.predict(X_test)

# Convert predictions to a readable format
fields = ['HKONT', 'KOSTL', 'Profitcenters', 'Paymentterms', 'Partnerbanktype', 'Taxcode']
prediction_dict = dict(zip(fields, predictions[0]))
print(prediction_dict)

entry.title: Invoke Generative AI Hub Models using @sap-ai-sdk/ai-api library in BAS
entry.content:
Hello Experts

I am working with the @SAP-ai-sdk/ai-api library to connect to a configured Generative AI Hub and query the available models. However, I'm having trouble identifying the correct API or function within the SDK to use in order to retrieve the results.

 

 

 

 

 

async function main() {
    try {

        const { GenerativeAIHubClient } = await import('@sap-ai-sdk/ai-api'); // Dynamic import
        const client = new GenerativeAIHubClient({
            uaa: {
                url: process.env.GENAI_HUB_TOKEN_URL, // UAA URL from service key
                clientId: process.env.GENAI_HUB_CLIENT_ID, // Client ID from service key
                clientSecret: process.env.GENAI_HUB_CLIENT_SECRET, // Client secret from service key
            },
            baseUrl: process.env.GENAI_HUB_URL, // Base URL from service key or env variable.
        });

        // List Models
        const models = await client.listModels();
        console.log("Available Models:", models);

        // Invoke Model
        const invokeResponse = await GenerativeAIHubClient.invokeModel({
            modelId: 'sap/generative-text', // Model ID
            prompt: "Explain SAP BTP in simple terms."
        });
        console.log("AI Response:", invokeResponse.output);

    } catch (error) {
        console.error("Error:", error);
        if (error.response) {
            console.error("Response Data:", error.response.data);
        }
    }
}

main();

 

 

 

 

Getting this error as this api is not available in the sdk export list.

 

 

 

 

 

ERROR    TypeError: GenerativeAIHubClient is not a constructor

 

 

 

 

Could someone please guide me on the proper method/function/approach (Not using Python) to invoke and get the desired output from the Generative AI Hub?

@TomFrenken @MirelaB @ZhongpinWang @deekshasinha

entry.title: Read timeouts from deployed GPT-4
entry.content:
Hi,

I recently encountered a problem that is a blocker for my functionality. 
Some time ago I deployed a GPT-4 from Generative AI Models in SAP AI Launchpad on BTP environment and it worked well at first, but lately it started throwing read timeouts from time to time. I would say five out of ten tries giving me error like below:
"I/O error on POST request for "<url>/chat/completions": Read timed out; nested exception is java.net.SocketTimeoutException: Read timed out "

I need to mention that problem doesn't exist working with GPT-3.5 and the same prompt.
But for functionality purposes I need more reasonable answers than from GPT-3.5.

Is there anyone who encountered the same problem or have any tips/solutions?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: A Successful Go-Live: Key to Project Success
entry.content:
The Cutover Plan: The Heart of Go-Live

The cutover plan is a detailed roadmap for transitioning from the old to the new system landscape. It includes all critical activities, timelines, and responsibilities. A well-thought-out cutover plan ensures that all technical and organizational tasks integrate smoothly. The key components of a cutover plan include:

  1. Data Migration – Clean and complete transfer of master and transactional data to the new system.

  2. System and Integration Testing – Ensuring that all relevant processes and interfaces function correctly.

  3. Smoketest – A quick initial functionality test immediately after go-live to verify that core system processes are operational.

  4. User and Authorization Management – Assigning and verifying roles for end users.

  5. Communication and Training – End users must be trained in using the new system to prevent errors in productive operation.

  6. Fallback Scenarios – A contingency plan in case issues arise that require reverting to the old system.

Customer Involvement: The Key to Success

A successful go-live requires not only technical expertise but also close collaboration with the customer. The following aspects are essential:

  • Engagement and Ownership: The customer must be actively involved in the processes and take ownership of their data and workflows.

  • Key Users and Training: Well-trained key users act as multipliers, ensuring that knowledge remains within the organization.

  • Data Quality: High-quality master data is essential for a smooth system operation. Customer validation of data is therefore crucial.

  • Effective Communication: Clear communication channels among all stakeholders prevent misunderstandings and ensure a seamless transition.

Post-Go-Live: Stabilization and Support

A successful go-live does not mark the end of the project. The first weeks after production start are crucial for system stability. A hypercare phase with intensive support ensures smooth operation and allows for quick corrections in case of issues. Regular feedback sessions help identify and implement optimizations early on.

Conclusion

A structured cutover plan and active customer involvement are the two most important factors for a successful go-live. Early customer engagement, thorough planning, and intensive support during the stabilization phase contribute to a seamless system transition and enable the benefits of the new system to be realized quickly.

entry.title: Cannot log into AI Launchpad
entry.content:
Hi 

I am having trouble logging into AI Launchpad. I am following this: Use Boosters for Free Tier Use of SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad | SAP Tutorials because I want to attempt this: Chatbot Development Made Easy with SAP AI Core and Build Apps 

I've done everything exactly the same using the free tier of BTP (not trial) and even tried other regions for the sub account. When trying to access the launchpad, I get to the login screen which says I cannot be authenticated. When I click forget password it doesn't send me a reset link leading to me thinking it can't find an account with my details.

entry.title: IDEAS FOR SAP
entry.content:
Hi guys,

I wanted to share an idea about two new SAP product concepts that I think could really add value if developed together: a self-driving enterprise core and an assistant

A self driving enterprise core is focused on system performance. It’s like a self-driving engine for SAP that can monitor, optimize, and even adjust workflows automatically. It helps businesses run more efficiently by reducing manual work and catching problems before they happen.

An assistant focuses on people. It helps users present their work better, communicate more clearly, and share insights easily—especially through integration with Meta Workplace. It’s about making individuals more productive, confident, and visible in their roles.

I believe SAP needs both. Self driving enterprise core improves how the system runs. An assistant improves how people show up and work within it. Together, they offer a balanced approach.

Thanks for reading—happy to talk more if there’s interest.

entry.title: AI use for reading existing SAP documentation to find the needed config solution
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

Pls could you advise AI tool which could search in existing SAP experts blogs, OSS notes and other documentation and help to  find correct answer to questions?

 

Example: #Dispute management documentation has over 1000 pages. But I have only some few hours to find answer to question:@How often dispute case comes do collection specialist work list and where to configure the frequency? 

I would like to ask the above question to #AI tool , which can search in OSS and other SAP documents.

 

Thank you

Best regards

Marcela

entry.title: Leveraging AI-Driven Test Automation for SAP Commerce Cloud Optimization
entry.content:
Why SAP Commerce Cloud Testing?

With the growing adoption of SAP Commerce Cloud for e-commerce, maintaining system performance and reliability has become critical. Due to the platform’s complexity—frequent updates, integrations, and diverse user requirements—SAP Commerce Cloud testing becomes increasingly critical.

With SAP Commerce Cloud's numerous integrations, thorough testing is needed to guarantee seamless functionality across all components. And, as e-commerce operations scale, the demand for continuous, efficient testing grows bigger still.

Consequently, manual testing is no longer sufficient to keep pace with these evolving demands. However, AI-driven SAP Commerce Cloud testing offers a solution. By leveraging AI, businesses can optimize platform performance, reduce time-to-market, and enhance overall reliability while ensuring a high-quality user experience.

The Challenges of SAP Commerce Cloud Testing

Traditional testing methods often fall short when it comes to handling the scale, complexity, and rapid changes associated with SAP Commerce Cloud environments. Some of the most prevalent challenges are:

High Volume of Transactions: E-commerce platforms handle a massive volume of transactions, making manual or conventional testing impractical.

Frequent Updates: Regular updates, customizations, and feature enhancements in the SAP Commerce Cloud demand frequent testing to ensure zero disruptions to services.

Complex Integrations: SAP Commerce Cloud integrates with numerous third-party systems like payment gateways, CRM tools, and inventory management systems, which increases testing complexity.

User Experience: Ensuring a consistent and bug-free user experience across a wide range of devices, browsers, and networks requires thorough testing at scale.

Regression Testing: With the constant deployment of new features, regression testing is necessary to ensure that updates do not break existing functionalities.

These challenges require a robust, scalable, and efficient testing solution capable of adapting to the evolving needs of modern e-commerce platforms.

Enter AI-Driven Test Automation

AI is revolutionizing how organizations approach testing, offering new opportunities for optimizing SAP Commerce Cloud environments. AI-driven test automation leverages machine learning, natural language processing (NLP), and other advanced algorithms to significantly improve the efficiency and accuracy of testing processes. Unlike traditional methods, which rely on predefined scripts and manual intervention, AI-based testing solutions can autonomously identify issues, predict potential failure points, and adapt to new changes in real time.

How AI-Driven Test Automation Works

AI-powered test automation for SAP Commerce Cloud works through several key features that make it highly effective:

Intelligent Test Generation: AI tools have the capability to autonomously create test cases by analyzing application behavior and leveraging historical testing data. By analyzing past test outcomes and learning from them, AI systems can create tests that cover a broader range of scenarios, including edge cases that might otherwise be missed.

Dynamic Test Maintenance: As SAP Commerce Cloud environments evolve, traditional test scripts often require manual updates. AI-driven solutions can autonomously update tests when the application undergoes changes or when new features are introduced, reducing the overhead of test maintenance.

Enhanced Test Coverage: AI-powered testing can automatically detect gaps in test coverage by analyzing the system architecture and usage patterns. This ensures that all critical functionalities are tested, regardless of how frequently the platform changes.

Regression Testing: One of the most time-consuming aspects of testing is regression testing, which ensures that new changes do not negatively impact existing functionality. AI-based automation tools can intelligently prioritize regression tests based on the likelihood of change, focusing resources on areas of the application most at risk of failure.

Faster Execution and Feedback: AI-driven test automation tools are capable of executing tests at a significantly faster rate than manual testing. This speed is crucial for organizations looking to deploy frequent updates or rapidly respond to customer feedback.

Predictive Analytics: AI solutions can predict areas of the SAP Commerce Cloud platform that are most likely to fail or require attention, based on historical test data. This proactive strategy allows businesses to address potential issues before they affect users.

Key Benefits of AI-Driven Test Automation for SAP Commerce Cloud Optimization

The integration of AI into SAP Commerce Cloud testing offers several compelling benefits that directly contribute to platform optimization:

1. Faster Time-to-Market

AI-driven test automation accelerates the testing process, allowing businesses to deploy new features and updates faster. This is crucial in an e-commerce landscape where speed and agility are key differentiators. By reducing the time spent on manual testing and troubleshooting, organizations can roll out innovations quicker, gaining a competitive edge.

2. Cost Savings

Although the upfront cost of AI-driven testing tools may appear high, the long-term savings are considerable. By automating repetitive and time-consuming tasks, businesses can reduce the need for manual testing resources. Furthermore, the reduced risk of bugs or system failures means fewer emergency fixes, leading to lower operational costs.

3. Improved Test Accuracy

AI systems learn from past tests and continuously improve their testing strategies, leading to more accurate and comprehensive testing. This reduces the likelihood of bugs slipping through the cracks and ensures that any issues are caught early in the development cycle.

4. Scalability

As businesses scale their SAP Commerce Cloud environments to handle higher volumes of traffic and transactions, AI-driven testing can easily adapt to the increased complexity. These tools are designed to scale with the platform, ensuring consistent performance testing regardless of the size of the e-commerce operation.

5. Seamless Integration with CI/CD Pipelines

AI-based test automation tools integrate seamlessly with CI/CD pipelines, allowing businesses to implement automated testing at every stage of the software development lifecycle. This ensures that testing becomes a seamless part of the development process, reducing bottlenecks and improving overall workflow efficiency.

6. Enhanced User Experience

Ultimately, the goal of testing is to deliver a flawless user experience. By using AI to identify issues early and test across a wide range of devices and platforms, businesses can ensure that customers enjoy a smooth, bug-free experience across all touchpoints.

Real-World Applications of AI-Driven Test Automation in SAP Commerce Cloud

Leading organizations are already leveraging AI-driven test automation to optimize their SAP Commerce Cloud platforms. For example, companies in the retail and manufacturing sectors have deployed AI-based testing tools to streamline the testing of customer-facing websites, ensuring a seamless shopping experience across multiple channels. Similarly, organizations in the financial services industry are using AI-driven testing to validate integrations with back-end systems, such as payment processors and CRM tools, ensuring that all parts of the SAP Commerce Cloud ecosystem work together without issues.

In addition, AI-based solutions can help improve accessibility testing for customers with disabilities, ensuring compliance with legal requirements and enhancing the overall customer experience.

Conclusion

The adoption of SAP Commerce Cloud provides businesses with powerful tools to enhance their e-commerce operations, but ensuring optimal performance across complex environments requires robust and efficient testing strategies. AI-driven test automation offers a cutting-edge solution for overcoming the challenges of traditional testing methods, enabling organizations to accelerate their time-to-market, reduce costs, and deliver a superior user experience.

By leveraging AI to automate and optimize the testing process, businesses can ensure that their SAP Commerce Cloud platforms remain reliable, scalable, and capable of meeting the demands of modern consumers. With continuous advancements in AI and machine learning, the future of SAP Commerce Cloud testing is not just about detecting errors but predicting and preventing them—leading to more innovative and successful e-commerce solutions.

entry.title: SAP AI CORE Free Tier Service
entry.content: Does SAP AI Core Free Tier provide any SAP AI units and how many? Can we use this free tier to train and deploy a machine learning model or is it just limited to exploring SAP AI Core? Also is there a way to explore all these SAP AI service and get hands on practice for free?

entry.title: "Failed to get org and user" error for SAP Commerce Visual Search
entry.content:
I'm trying to intergate Visual Search API with SAP Commerce Cloud.
But "/images/v2/search" API is failing with 401 Unauthorized error and message: Failed to get org and user

All other APIs from SAP AI Toolkit are working fine with same Bearer token.

entry.title: Full Stack ABAP Application In Build
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to make a full stack ABAP Information using SAP Build For using AI Features like :

1) ABAP Business Object Generation.

2) ABAP Joule 

But Facing Some issues, Please Provide some assistance.

entry.title: AI units license in Sales Demo for SuccessFactors Recruiting MS Teams integration
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

we have a commercialized Sales Demo (SFCPART) and are using it mainly for Demos in SuccessFactors Recruiting. We would like to configure "Generating Interview Questions", which is part of the interviews functionality with the integration of Microsoft Teams. One prerequisite is the AI units license.

Is it possible to acquire the AI units license for a Sales Demo and is the mentioned feature available in this environment?

 

Many thanks,

Judith
